mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals a tightening labor market that reduces the supply of labor, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce negative returns in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 1.4 million units signals an increase in demand for housing, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in housing boom periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce positive returns in at least 70% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below 100 signals a decrease in supply chain capacity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in supply chain disruption periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce negative returns in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce negative returns in at least 70% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decrease in payroll employment below 150 million signals a decrease in labor market participation, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in labor market contraction periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce negative returns in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 240 million signals an increase in demand for goods and services, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in demand boom periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce positive returns in at least 70% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 240 million and a 3-month increase in industrial production above 100 signals a simultaneous increase in demand and supply chain capacity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in simultaneous demand and supply chain boom periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce positive returns in at least 60% of triggered instances.
